stages.name: Environment Setup
stages.description: Create repository, install dependencies, download datasets and models
actions: Create project skeleton with directories src/, data/, configs/, scripts/, models/, results/
Install required Python packages from requirements.txt (torch, transformers, datasets, rouge_score, sacrebleu, scipy, openai, google-generativeai)
Download XL-Sum dataset for all languages using datasets library, save to data/xlsum/
Download HeSum Hebrew dataset from source and extract into data/hesum/
Set environment variables OPENAI_API_KEY and GEMINI_API_KEY in .env file
Download pre-trained models (mBERT, language-specific BERTs, wmt22-comet-da) into models/pretrained/

stages.name: Train Baselines (Plan)
stages.description: Implement and train all baseline methods for comparison — plan phase
actions: Create config/languages.yaml listing 8 languages with typological family and resource level assignment
Document sampling strategy: random seed 42, 400 articles per language from test splits in config/sampling.yaml
Write prompt templates for GPT-3.5 and Gemini summarization in configs/prompts/summarization.txt
Design MTurk HIT document: qualification question, instructions, 1-4 Likert scales for coherence/completeness in docs/mturk_design.md
Define metrics plan in configs/metrics.yaml listing all n-gram and neural metrics with tokenizer settings
Outline correlation analysis approach: Pearson between scores and human ratings, grouping, significance in analysis_plan.md

stages.name: Train Baselines (Impl)
stages.description: Implement and train all baseline methods for comparison — impl phase
actions: Implement src/data/language_selection.py to load languages.yaml and provide language list
Write src/data/sample_articles.py to load datasets, sample 400 articles per language with seed 42
Implement src/generation/generate_summaries.py with functions calling OpenAI and Gemini APIs
Create src/corruption/coherence.py and src/corruption/completeness.py implementing strategies from design doc
Develop MTurk code: src/annotation/create_hits.py and process_results.py to manage HIT creation and retrieval
Implement src/metrics/ngram_metrics.py for ROUGE-1/2/3/L, BLEU, CHRF with default, mBERT, monolingual tokenizers
Implement src/metrics/neural_metrics.py for MoverScore, BERTScore, COMET, Gemini-as-judge
Write src/analysis/aggregate_human_ratings.py to average 3 annotator scores per summary

stages.name: Train Baselines (Exec)
stages.description: Implement and train all baseline methods for comparison — exec phase
actions: Run python src/data/sample_articles.py --config config/sampling.yaml to produce data/sampled_articles.json
Run python src/generation/generate_summaries.py --input data/sampled_articles.json --output data/system_summaries.jsonl
Run python src/corruption/apply_corruptions.py --input data/system_summaries.jsonl --output data/corrupted_summaries/
Launch MTurk HITs via MTurk API using src/annotation/launch_hits.py, then fetch results with fetch_results.py saving to data/mturk_raw.csv
Run python src/analysis/aggregate_human_ratings.py --input data/mturk_raw.csv --output data/human_ratings.json
Run n-gram metric computation: python src/metrics/compute_all_metrics.py --metric ngram --summaries data/system_summaries.jsonl --references data/references.json --output data/ngram_scores.csv
Run neural metric computation: python src/metrics/compute_all_metrics.py --metric neural --summaries data/system_summaries.jsonl --references data/references.json --output data/neural_scores.csv
Combine all metric scores into data/metrics_scores.csv using merge script

stages.name: Train Proposed Method
stages.description: Implement and train the proposed method
actions: Load human ratings (data/human_ratings.json) and system summaries (data/system_summaries.jsonl) in data preparation
Split data into training/validation/test sets using src/data/split_data.py
Train regression models to predict human ratings from metric scores using src/analysis/train_model.py
Perform correlation analysis between metrics and human ratings per language and corruption type using src/analysis/correlation.py
Store trained models in models/trained/ and results in results/tables/

stages.name: Evaluation
stages.description: Run evaluation on all experiments and compute metrics
actions: Run trained regression models on held-out test set to obtain predicted human ratings
Compute final Pearson/Spearman correlations between predicted and actual human ratings by language, corruption, and overall
Perform statistical significance tests (e.g., bootstrap confidence intervals) on correlation differences
Generate comparison plots (bar charts, scatter plots) of metric correlations for report
Compile final leaderboard tables ranking metrics and regression models across evaluation dimensions
Save all evaluation outputs to results/evaluation/

stages.name: Final Verification
stages.description: Compare reproduced results against paper claims
actions: Archive all result files, trained models, and logs into results/archive/
Write final project report summarizing methodology, results, and conclusions
Verify reproducibility by re-running a random subset of experiments from scratch using the archived scripts and data
Clean up temporary files and terminate API resources
Submit final deliverables as per project requirements
